dataset.description: This dataset provides comprehensive agricultural data for Rajasthan, India from 2018 to 2019. It contains four tabless: Crop_Production_Data, Water_Usage_Data, Soil_Analysis_Data, and Crop_Price_Data. This dataset will be a valuable resource for researchers and data scientists working in the agricultural sector, enabling them to analyze and model various aspects of agricultural practices and market dynamics in Rajasthan.

columns.description: Administrative region in Rajasthan where crop prices are recorded
columns.name: District

columns.description: Type of agricultural produce being traded
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Specific APMC (Agricultural Produce Market Committee) mandi/marketplace where transactions occur
columns.name: Market

columns.description: Transaction date in YYYY-MM-DD format
columns.name: Date

columns.description: Market rate per quintal (100kg) of crop in Indian Rupees
columns.name: Price (INR/quintal)

columns.description: Geographical region within Rajasthan cultivating the crop
columns.name: District

columns.description: Agricultural commodity grown
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Growing season classification: Kharif (monsoon) or Rabi (winter)
columns.name: Season

columns.description: Land extent allocated for crop cultivation
columns.name: Area (hectares)

columns.description: Agricultural output per unit area
columns.name: Yield (quintals)

columns.description: Total crop output accounting for yield and cultivated area
columns.name: Production (metric tons)

columns.description: Administrative zone with distinct soil characteristics
columns.name: District

columns.description: Classification based on geological composition (e.g., black lava, saline)
columns.name: Soil Type

columns.description: Acidity/alkalinity measurement critical for crop suitability
columns.name: pH Level

columns.description: Percentage of decomposed plant/animal material affecting soil fertility
columns.name: Organic Matter (%)

columns.description: Essential nutrient level for plant growth in kilograms per hectare
columns.name: Nitrogen Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Key mineral content influencing root development and energy transfer
columns.name: Phosphorus Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Vital nutrient level for water regulation and disease resistance
columns.name: Potassium Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Administrative region tracking water resource utilization
columns.name: District

columns.description: Agricultural product associated with water consumption
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Water distribution technique (e.g., drip, sprinkler, canal)
columns.name: Irrigation Method

columns.description: Actual water usage per hectare for crop cultivation
columns.name: Water Consumption (liters/hectare)

columns.description: Accessible water resources per hectare for agricultural use
columns.name: Water Availability (liters/hectare)

desc: This dataset provides comprehensive agricultural data for Rajasthan, India from 2018 to 2019. It contains four tabless: Crop_Production_Data, Water_Usage_Data, Soil_Analysis_Data, and Crop_Price_Data. This dataset will be a valuable resource for researchers and data scientists working in the agricultural sector, enabling them to analyze and model various aspects of agricultural practices and market dynamics in Rajasthan.
goal: Use crop production data (including crop type, season, area, yield) to analyze crop productivity in different districts and seasons, predict future yield trends, and support decision-making in agricultural production.
name: Visual Analytics System agriculture_goal2_1742653097

plan.path: The user starts with View 1 (Overview) analyzing seasonal yield trends and making forecasts. After setting a prediction horizon, they explore View 2 (Exploration) to compare district-level yields for selected crops. Clicking a district bar activates View 3 (Exploration) showing water-soil efficiency relationships. A brush selection on View 3 finally reveals View 4 (Insight) clustering districts by production characteristics.

views.components: Range slider for forecast horizon (1-5 years)

effect.description: Updates ARIMA model horizon and confidence bands

visualization.description: Multi-line chart showing seasonal yield trends with ARIMA forecast overlay. Kharif/Rabi seasons form x-axis. Solid lines represent historical data; dashed lines show predictions.

effect.description: Filters subsequent views to selected district-crop pairs

visualization.description: Small multiples of horizontal bar charts sorted by district yield averages with ±1σ error bars

effect.description: Highlights districts meeting selected efficiency criteria

visualization.description: Scatter plot showing water-use efficiency with soil type-based coloring
Parallel coordinates plot showing production efficiency clusters

data.reason: Crop_price_data.Date reflects market transactions unrelated to production timelines - seasons better align with yield cycles and simplify trend calculations
data.requirement: Calculate quarterly/annual yield growth rates using production season cycles (Kharif=Monsoon, Rabi=Winter) instead of price data dates

interaction.reason: Hexbin brushing incompatible with aggregated bins - standard selection better maintains coordinated filtering
interaction.requirement: Point/interval selection on scatter plots to highlight correlating districts in line/bar charts

interaction.reason: Confidence band adjustment requires statistical backend unavailable in client-side Vega-Lite - horizon control is implementable
interaction.requirement: Slider to adjust forecast horizon (years ahead) in trend visualizations via parametric queries

visualization.reason: Box plots disallow interactions critical for coordinated views and faceted crops (23 types) would create visual clutter
visualization.requirement: Bar charts showing average yield per crop type with error bars, grouped by district

visualization.reason: Hexbin not supported in Vega-Lite combo charts and categorical overlays require explicit point visibility for interaction
visualization.requirement: Scatter plot with aggregated mean Yield vs Water Consumption (liters/hectare) using soil type as color encoding

tasks.method: Multi-line chart grouped by season (Kharif/Rabi) showing annual yield trends. Temporal aggregation uses season cycles instead of exact dates. Compute growth rates between seasons using production data. Enable faceting by crop type.
tasks.objective: Identify cyclical productivity patterns and quantify growth trends aligned with Rajasthan's agricultural calendar

tasks.method: Small multiples of horizontal bar charts (one per crop) with district sorting. Encode average yield as bar length, error bars showing ±1σ. Enable click-to-drill into district-level time series.
tasks.objective: Compare baseline yields and variability across districts for strategic crop allocation

tasks.method: Scatter plot with yield-water ratio on X-axis, absolute yield on Y-axis, soil type as color. Overlay trend lines per soil category. Brush selection syncs with Task 2's bar charts via district filtering.
tasks.objective: Analyze water-use efficiency relationships in different soil conditions

tasks.method: ARIMA-based forecast line overlaid on Task 1's trend chart. Slider (1-5 years) controls forecast horizon. Confidence bands updated via parametric backend query on slider interaction.
tasks.objective: Support long-term agricultural planning through yield predictions

tasks.method: Connected scatter plot showing yield deviations (z-scores) vs district averages. Anomalies highlighted via dynamic threshold (2σ). Click anomalies to populate comparison views with normal cases.
tasks.objective: Detect districts needing intervention due to abnormal productivity patterns

tasks.method: Parallel coordinates plot linking normalized production metrics. Implement k-means clustering (k=3) to color-code districts by production efficiency archetypes.
tasks.objective: Identify district groups for targeted resource allocation strategies
